text:
Q: Where can I download my EC ticket?
A: The ticket purchased for EC can only be downloaded from the EC account from which it was purchased, from My Account -> My Orders.

Q: I have the ticket in PDF format, but I can't find it in my EC account, is that okay?
A: If you already have it in PDF format and have all the data on it, then it's good to go . Camping

Q: If I have a festival + Camping subscription, do I receive a tent from you?
A: If you have a festival pass that includes Camping, you can come with your own tent or purchase an accommodation structure from the site.

Q: I can't find any accommodation structures to purchase. Will they really no longer be available?
A: At the moment, the only available options for purchasing a tent can be found on the exchange, the ones on our website are sold out. From there you can purchase an accommodation structure, or you can opt to bring your own tent.

Q: I want to know more about what types of tents we can bring with us, can you tell me if there are any limitations?
A: There are no size limitations for personal tents. We recommend that festival-goers bring tents appropriate for the number of people who will be staying in them. Access with awnings of any type is not permitted.

Q: Where can I pitch my tent?
A: There will be a specially designated area for personal tents. Campsite volunteers will guide you to find a suitable spot to pitch your tent.

Q: Can I get a campsite for just 3 days of the festival?
A: Access to the Camping area is only allowed for festival season ticket holders, not day tickets. You can stay as long as you want in the Camping, until the end of the festival.

Q: What does Car Camping mean?
A: The Car Camping ticket allows one car to enter the camping area. In a 3x8 meter space, you will be able to park your car and pitch your personal tent. Only one Car Camping ticket is required, but all people in the car will need a festival pass and camping pass. A maximum of 5 people are allowed in the car.

Q: Is it okay if I exceed the 3x8 meter space?
A: We recommend that participants do not significantly exceed the lines drawn for each space. If the delimited spaces are exceeded, an additional space will be needed for the placement of tents and, implicitly, an additional Car Camping ticket.

Q: What does RV Camping mean?
A: The RV Camping ticket allows access to one RV/motorhome in the camping area. There will be a specially arranged area for RV parking. Only one RV Camping ticket is required, but all people who will be staying in this RV will need a festival subscription and camping pass. Details can be found here: https://electriccastle.ro/ec-village Minor situations

Q: Do I need to buy a ticket for my 7-year-old son?
A: Minors up to and including 7 years old have free access throughout the festival, if accompanied by a parent/legal guardian who holds a valid season ticket. The adult accompanying the minor must complete the following declaration: https://electriccastle.ro/assets/docs/declaratie-minori-sub-16ani.pdf

Q: If I am 16 years old, can I come to the festival without being accompanied by a parent?
A: Minors between 16 and 18 years old can come to the festival unaccompanied. A parent/legal guardian will be required to complete the following declaration: https://electriccastle.ro/assets/docs/declaratie-minori-peste-16ani.pdf, which the minor must present upon entry to the festival.

Q: I purchased a ticket for my 7-year-old daughter, only then did I see that she has free access to the festival. Can I get a refund?
A: Hello! We understand the situation, but tickets already purchased are not refundable, according to EC regulations. Exchange

Q: How can I change the data on a ticket?
A: Through our Exchange platform, you can change the data on an EC ticket. The data can only be changed from the EC account to which the ticket belongs, and an exchange fee is charged for this change, as communicated in the EC general regulations.

Q: I can't make it to the festival and I can't sell my ticket. Can I get my money back?
A: Once purchased on the EC website, tickets and subscriptions are non-refundable. According to the regulations, purchased tickets cannot be replaced, and their value can only be refunded through the Exchange Platform, according to the provisions of III.3. You can sell your tickets to people interested in participating in the EC, exclusively using the Exchange platform. You can also find out how the platform works in the regulations.

Q: Could I change the dates or sell a Youth Pass 21 ticket?
A: The Youth Pass 21 ticket cannot be sold or offered to another person, according to the regulations: https://electriccastle.ro/youth-pass-21

Q: I purchased a Car Camping ticket, but we will be staying at a hotel after all. Can I get my money back for the Car Camping ticket?
A: For this, we make our Exchange platform available, this being the only option available for tickets, structures or facilities already purchased.

Q: How do I know that I will receive my ticket purchased through Exchange?
A: According to the regulations, any transaction regarding tickets within the Exchange Platform will be made strictly between the ticket holder and the buyer, EC not having the quality of an intermediary or party in the understanding and/or transaction of the parties. You must ensure that the seller has followed all the steps in the instructions and has finally pressed the Send to buyer button.

Q: How does the Exchange platform work, what steps need to be followed?
A: Here you can find how the Exchange platform works: [link needed] Through the platform, transfer the ticket to another person. When the seller generates the exchange link, the buyer accesses it and will have the opportunity to modify the data on the ticket, paying the processing fee (15 euros). Even if the ticket still remains in the seller's account, it will have the nominal data on it, the seller having to press the SEND TO BUYER button, for the transfer to be completed, and the ticket to reach the buyer's account. In this way, the respective seller will not be able to transfer the same ticket more than once, to multiple accounts, but only once, to a single account, that of the buyer. The tickets are nominal, so as long as the name and address on the ticket do not match those of the person who will actually use the ticket at the festival, that person will not be able to enter the festival. If you ever have an unpleasant experience with a seller on the platform, just send us an email and let us know, and we will try to help you as soon as possible. Use of the EC logo / Collaboration proposals

Q: Can I use the EC logo on the contest visual I organize?
A: Organizing competitions with tickets or invitations to EC is strictly prohibited by the regulations, and using the EC logo or image without an established agreement is strictly prohibited. If you are interested in a collaboration, you can send an email to our colleagues who deal with partners: sponsors@electriccastle.ro.

Q: I'm interested in a collaboration. Who could I talk to?
A: You can leave us an email at sponsors@electriccastle.ro. Our colleagues will get back to you with more details.

Q: We are organizing the Freshman Ball and we want to offer 2 EC subscriptions as a prize. Are you interested in this collaboration?
A: Thank you very much for the proposal! We are not interested at the moment. Also, organizing contests and giveaways with tickets and invitations is a benefit that we grant strictly to festival partners. Failure to comply with this aspect of the regulation may lead to the cancellation of tickets/invitations, and we don't want that. External partners

Q: If I purchased a ticket from iabilet.ro, is it valid for EC?
A: All tickets purchased from partner sites are valid. As they are not tickets purchased from the EC site, we cannot verify them in our ticketing system, but if you need confirmation, please contact our partners to confirm the validity of your tickets.

Q: Could I purchase a ticket on olx, and not on the EC website?
A: EC tickets can only be purchased from our website or our partner websites. A full list of authorized ticketing partners can be found here: https://electriccastle.com/tickets-info Change data / Change ID card

Q: I entered the wrong name of the person who owns the ticket and I can't edit it now. Can you help me?
A: For small typos, you can write us an email at contact@electriccastle.ro. If more than 48 hours have passed since the ticket was purchased, another person's data can only be transferred to the ticket via the Exchange platform.

Q: I recently changed my ID and I can't edit the data on my ticket, what can I do about this?
A: Hello! Leave us your current ID card details, we will help you with the change in the system.

Q: I lost my ID card a few days ago, how could I access the festival then?
A: You will be able to access the festival area with another legal document that shows your details (name and address), such as a passport/driving license or birth certificate. Youth Pass 21

Q: What exactly are Youth Pass 21 tickets? Do I qualify if I am 21 years and 8 months old during the festival?
A: Youth Pass 21 tickets are festival subscriptions, at a great price, for all people aged 21 - more precisely, who do not turn 22 by the end of the festival.

Q: I didn't know that children under 7 have free access, so I purchased a Youth Pass for my son. Could I get my money back or buy a tent with that money?
A: Transfer of ticket value for other EC ticket types/offers is not possible, and tickets already purchased are non-refundable, according to the regulations. Please leave us more details, and we will look for a solution in this regard.

Q: I purchased a Youth Pass for my 7-year-old daughter. Then I found out that she gets free entry to the festival. Could I transfer the ticket to a friend of hers?
A: Tickets from the Youth Pass 21 offer are not transferable and cannot be sold through our Exchange platform, according to the regulations: https://electriccastle.ro/youth-pass-21 . Disabled people

Q: How do I get the free invitation? I am a disabled person.
A: Hello! To obtain the ticket, the eligible person must complete this form: https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/accespersoanecuhandicaptec2025 accompanied by supporting documents.

Q: I understand that I also receive a free invitation for the person accompanying me, is that true?
A: Companions benefit from a free subscription for the 5 days of the festival, General Pass type without camping.

Q: I haven't received the invitation yet. Is everything okay? (invalid document)
A: We have now checked, the document you sent is no longer valid/needs to be revised. We will need an updated document, then we will be able to send you the festival invitation and the discount to the companion. volunteer

Q: I haven't received any response regarding my application for Volunteering at EC, is everything okay?
A: Please write to our colleagues at volunteers@electriccastle.ro about this situation. They will be able to help you in this regard.

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (SOON TO BE OPEN)
A: The official call for volunteers will be opened in the spring, it will be posted on social media pages and on the website. Stay tuned!

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (ALREADY OPEN)
A: The call for Volunteers is now open. Find more details here: electriccastle.ro/volunteers

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (CLOSED)
A: The call for Volunteers is currently closed, but we will be able to add you to an additional list. Drop us an email at contact@electriccastle.ro with all the necessary details and we will forward your message to the Volunteer Coordinators. Thanks!

Q: I would need a volunteer certificate after volunteering at Electric Castle, can you help me?
A: You can contact our colleagues from the EC Volunteer team for more details. Here is the email address: volunteers@electriccastle.ro Parking

Q: How far is the festival parking lot?
A: The EC Parking Space is a 5-10 minute walk from the festival entrance gates.

Q: What is the difference between EC parking and Car Camping?
A: The Car Camping area is part of the EC Village, while the EC parking lot is outside this area. The EC parking lot is available for participants who come to the festival by car and who do not want to stay in the EC Village. Details on how to access it will be announced before the festival. In the Car Camping, participants will be able to enter by car and set up their tents in specially arranged areas. You can find the regulations here

Q: I have accommodation in Bontida, how will I be able to park my car?
A: You will need to contact your host in Bontida. They will send the license plate numbers of your car to the City Hall. You will then have access to the main road in Bontida and will have to park in the yard. The police will report cars that are not parked or parked improperly and will apply sanctions.

Q: I will only know on the first day of the festival whether I am coming by car or not, will there still be parking tickets available?
A: We recommend festival-goers to purchase their access tickets to the EC parking space in advance.

Q: Do I have somewhere to leave my bike if I come to the festival by bike?
A: Yes, you can leave your bike tied to the stand near the main entrance to the festival.

Q: Can I come to the festival by motorcycle?
A: We will announce more details about this before the festival. Invoices

Q: I would like to receive a tax invoice for my ticket order, is that possible?
A: You can request the tax invoice using the following link, from your EC account: www.electriccastle.ro/request-invoice or by email to our colleagues at financier@electriccastle.ro.

Q: I can't access that link and I need the invoice, what can I do?
A: Please leave an email to our colleagues at financier@electriccastle.ro and they will respond as soon as possible. CARDS

Q: My card has expired and I want to update my details to purchase tickets. How do I do this?
A: To replace the card, you need the following: 1. log in to your EC account, 2. go to the My Orders section, 3. click on the “Update credit card” button Invitations

Q: I lost my invitation code. What can I do about it?
A: According to the regulation, it is mentioned: In the case of Redeem Codes, their beneficiary, namely the person who received the redeem code, is solely responsible for its storage and use, regardless of whether it comes from Electric Castle or from partners. Losing the code or deleting the confirmation email may lead to the impossibility of use, and all damages arising from this circumstance remain the responsibility of the beneficiary. Electric Castle does not guarantee and is not liable for the loss of the code and the impossibility of its recovery or use.

Q: How could I turn the invitation code into a festival ticket?
A: You can activate the code on your invitation here: electriccastle.ro/redeem-access .

Q: I received an invitation, but I can't make it and I would like to transfer it to a friend, how can I do this?
A: According to EC regulations, invitations, once generated, become nominal and are not transferable, i.e. they cannot be sold or transferred through Exchange.

Q: Do I really have to pay the 50 lei donation for the invitation I have?
A: Guest Pass invitations can only be activated after the mandatory donation for Banffy Castle is paid. After that, the festival ticket will be generated in your EC account. Call for Craft Vendors (SOON TO BE OPEN/ ALREADY OPEN)

Q: Where can I apply with my handmade products or crafts? (SOON TO BE OPEN)
A: The official calls for EC11 will be given a little later. They will be available on social media pages and on the website, so we suggest you apply with confidence.

Q: Where can I apply with my handmade products or crafts? (ALREADY OPEN)
A: Hello! Below is the link to the form you need to fill out. Call for Food Vendors (SOON TO BE OPEN/ ALREADY OPEN)

Q: Where can I apply with my foodtruck? (SOON TO BE OPEN)
A: The official calls for EC11 will be given a little later. The form will be available on the website, we will also announce it on social media pages. Drop us an email at food@electriccastle.ro if you want to give us all the details .

Q: Where can I apply with my food truck? (ALREADY OPEN)
A: Hello! Below is the link to the form you need to fill out. Call for Vendors (CLOSED)

Q: I know the application deadline has passed, but can I still apply now?
A: The festival program is currently full. Thank you for your interest and we encourage you to apply for the calls for the next festival editions. Lineup

Q: Hello, are you adding any more artists to the EC lineup?
A: The lineup for EC is complete. Thanks!

Q: When will you announce the artists for the next edition?
A: The dates for the EC are not yet confirmed. The artistic program will be available later. Lost and Found

Q: I lost a pair of glasses at the festival. Can you help me with information?
A: Leave an email to our colleagues at lost@electriccastle.ro, they will help you with an answer in this regard.

Q: How good that you found my glasses! Where can I pick them up?
A: Can you please give us your delivery details? We will send the package by courier / You can pick it up in person in Cluj, at Str.Piezisa, no.19, between 10 am and 6 pm, Monday to Friday. Installments/Rates

Q: I paid the first installment for the EC ticket, but I had problems with my bank card. How can I continue paying for my order?
A: For each automatic withdrawal, you received an email notification regarding the deadline for the next installment to be paid. The order is now canceled, and the first installment paid cannot be recovered, according to the installment ticket regulations.

Q: I want to purchase an EC ticket in installments. How can I do that? (EC installment plan unavailable)
A: Currently, the only installment plan available on the site is the one offered through the BT Star card. The installments were available until December 20. Details about the installments will be announced on the site and social media pages.

Q: There's a problem with my card. Can I pay manually?
A: You can use the 'Update credit card details' button and use a new bank card. Manually, you will only be able to pay the remaining amount in full via 'Pay remainder amount' Structure/Product SOLD OUT

Q: I want to purchase a White Lodge, but it seems to me that it is out of stock. Are they still available or not?
A: Hey! They appear out of stock, but they are not sold out yet. These are added to the shopping cart of some people. They will receive a notification message with information about the completion of the order. Please check their availability on the site in 24 hours. / These are purchased in installments. Once the installments are successfully completed, the products will become sold out. Otherwise, they will be available again on the site.

Q: I see that the Youth Pass tickets are no longer available. Are they sold out already?
A: We will check with our colleagues immediately and get back to you with an answer. Youth Pass 21 tickets were available in limited numbers. Account Issues

Q: Hello, I can't log in to my EC account. Can you help me?
A: Send us an email at orders@electriccastle.ro and describe the problem a little. Our colleagues will help you as soon as possible.

Q: I can't remember my EC account password, and the "Forgot my password" button doesn't work. What can I do?
A: We have changed your password now. Please try logging in and then change your password to a new one. Internship/Job at EC

Q: I want to do an internship at Electric Castle. Do you have any positions available?
A: You can find the only available positions here: https://electriccastle.ro/careers We currently have no open internship positions. or The team is currently complete, but you can apply for a project-based role and we will get back to you when we need more people on the team to help us.

Q: Could I work during EC?
A: We currently do not have any positions available within the EC staff. You can apply to be part of the EC Bar team or to be a Cashier. Photographer/Videographer at the festival

Q: Hello! I'm a photographer and I would like to be part of the Electric Castle photography team for the next festival edition. How can I join the team?
A: The team of photographers is currently complete. If there is a need to complete it, the colleagues who coordinate the team will contact you. Thank you! Transport

Q: Hello! If we bought return tickets for a certain time, do we have to take that bus or can we take it at another time?
A: Hello! The time on the tickets is not relevant, they can be used at any time for a trip on the EC bus.

Q: Hello. For children under 12 (who have free entry to the festival) what are the conditions for bus transportation?
A: Hello! If they occupy a seat on the bus/train, it is necessary to purchase a regular ticket

Q: Hello! What are the operating hours for the buses? Are they non-stop or do they only leave at a certain time?
A: Hello! When the bus is full, it leaves. It operates non-stop. On Mondays at 12:00 the last bus leaves from Bonțida.

Q: Greetings. If there are still free seats on the train, can they be purchased on site? Because they can no longer be purchased online an hour in advance.
A: Hello! You can only purchase them online. If it helps, there is also the option of taking the bus. They run non-stop

Q: Hello! How long does it take for the EC buses to get to the festival?
A: Hello! The time is generally about half an hour, but during peak times it can reach an hour or even an hour and a half.

Q: Can bus tickets be picked up from the bus at any time?
A: Hello! They can be purchased online and from bus stations where there are dedicated counters. You will be able to pay with your festival bracelet or cash, you will not find POS at the Ticketing points. They cannot be taken from the bus.

Q: Where can I park my motorcycle? Is there dedicated parking? How much does it cost?
A: Hello! You can park in the large parking lot, where cars are also. The entrance is shared, there is no entrance fee and there is only a row dedicated to motorcycles.

Regulations
THESE RULES APPLY TO THE ELECTRIC CASTLE FESTIVAL, 2025 EDITION.
FESTIVAL DATES: JULY 16-20, 2025
The organizer of the Festival is SC Electricity Project SRL., headquartered in Cluj-Napoca, str. Piezișă no. 19, room. 6, Cluj county, registered with ONRC under no. J12/3193/2024, with CUI 50360413, legally represented by Mr. Mihai Alin PAUN, e-mail: contact@electriccastle.ro.
These Regulations apply to site users at the time of browsing the website, purchasing a ticket to participate in the Festival, respectively to participants from the moment of receiving the ticket until the date of the Festival, as well as during the Festival.
The Organizer reserves the right to modify the provisions of these Regulations unilaterally, without prior notice, which shall be immediately applicable from the moment of their publication on the website.

Participants can enter and exit the festival grounds whenever they want, provided they have an intact festival RFID bracelet on their wrist.
The starting times of the events within the Festival, as well as the order of the artists' representatives, may be unilaterally modified by the Organizer, without this modification being likely to cause any kind of damage to any Participant.
All participants are subject to body control upon entering the festival, in order to ensure the safety conditions necessary for the event. Refusal to undergo body control will result in the ban on access to the festival for any participant.
DEFINITIONS
Contactless MasterCard Bank Card – material element, issued by an accredited bank or institution which constitutes the method of collection/payment of goods sold/purchased inside the Event Venue, according to the Event Regulations.

RFID (RadioFrequency IDentification) Bracelet – the material element that constitutes the only means of collecting/paying for goods sold/purchased inside the Event Venue.
RFID Bracelet Loading – the process by which Event participants will load the "money" payment instrument onto the "RFID Bracelet" payment instrument, a tool with which they will be able to purchase products sold during the Event.
RFID Bracelet Refund – the reverse process of the "RFID Bracelet Recharge" process, namely the process by which Event participants will download the remaining unspent amounts from the "RFID Bracelet" payment instrument to the "money" payment instrument. The RFID Bracelet Refund process requires that the RFID Bracelet Recharge process initially exist.

RFID Bracelet Reader – the device-instrument used to manage the collection/payment of products sold/purchased.
The amount loaded on the RFID Bracelet is a multi-purpose voucher (MPV), as defined by European Commission legislation.
The festival territory is represented by the space in the courtyard and interior of Banffy Castle in Bonţida, Cluj County, where access is allowed to participants who have a valid ticket to the Electric Castle 2025 event, a space delimited according to the signs installed by the Organizer. It does not include the camping area.
The camping area is the accommodation space provided by the Organizer, near the festival area. This area is accessible to participants who have a valid camping pass for the Electric Castle 2025 event. It does not include the festival area.

Indoor area – It is any space that has a roof, ceiling or floor and is bounded by at least two walls, regardless of their nature or their temporary or permanent character.
Outdoor area – Any other space that is not considered indoor.
Website - www.electriccastle.com or www.electriccastle.ro

Products purchased from the festival shop are allowed in the camping area based on proof of product transaction. In the case of drinks, they must remain sealed.

I. GENERAL PROVISIONS
1. These regulations will also be referred to as Terms and Conditions (“TC”) and apply to the organization and conduct of the Electric Castle event (hereinafter referred to as the Festival), organized by Festival Ticket Management SRL (hereinafter referred to as the Organizer) in the demarcated area of Bonțida commune, Cluj county (hereinafter referred to as the Location).
2. The Regulation contains the provisions that each participant of the event must comply with and their obligations in relation to the Organizer and the other participants in the Festival.
3. These TC can be found on the website, more precisely at the URL: https://electriccastle.ro/terms-and-conditions , and will be displayed at the entrance to the Festival.

4. The Organizer reserves the right to modify these Regulations at any time, without prior notice to the Participant. Any modification of these Regulations will be posted on the event website www.electriccastle.com as well as at the entrance to the event venue, being immediately applicable from the moment of posting.
5. By purchasing a ticket and/or by accessing the Festival, or/and by creating an account on the electriccastle.com website, the Participant declares that he/she has read, understood and undertakes to comply with these Regulations.
II. TICKET CATEGORIES AND THEIR PURCHASE
1. TICKET CATEGORIES
1.1. Tickets can be purchased exclusively online, from the website www.electriccastle.com or from the websites of the Organizer's partners, according to the provisions of chapter III.2.

1.2. The organizer reserves the right to change the ticket price without prior notice.
1.3. Participants can opt for several types of tickets and ticket facilities, namely:
General Access Pass (festival pass for the entire duration of the festival)
General Access Pass + Eurolife insurance (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Premium Access Pass (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Premium Access Pass + Eurolife insurance (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Camping Pass (camping pass for the entire duration of the festival)
General Access Pass – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds, excluding the camping and Premium areas. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event.

General Access Pass + Eurolife insurance – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds, excluding the camping and Premium areas. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event. Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Premium Access Pass – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds and the Premium area mentioned on the festival map, for the entire duration of the event.
Premium Access Pass + Eurolife insurance – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds and the Premium area mentioned on the festival map, for the entire duration of the event. Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Camping Pass – This ticket provides access to the camping grounds, excluding the festival area. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event.

Participants will be able to opt for Camping passes and several types of camping accommodation. These are available here: electriccastle.com/tickets

Participants who purchase such tickets declare that they comply with the age requirements and assume that they will not be allowed access to the Festival if, upon ticket validation, they are found not to meet the requirements. The costs of festival and facility passes can be consulted on electriccastle.com/tickets .

* In addition, as part of the "Bonțida Fever" campaign held in the summer of 2023, the Organizer offered free Lifetime Pass tickets (for life) to participants who had a permanent black "Bonțida Fever" tattoo and communicated proof thereof to the Organizer by 26.06.2023. Henna tattoos, semi-permanent tattoos, stickers or any temporary drawings/graphics/paintings are not accepted. To the extent that the Organizer has a reasonable suspicion that the beneficiaries of the Lifetime Pass tickets have defrauded/failed to comply with the eligibility conditions presented above, in order to obtain the tickets in bad faith, the Organizer reserves the right to request the beneficiaries, upon access to the Festival and/or at the time of picking up the RFID bracelet, to present a current proof of the permanent "Bonțida Fever" tattoo. To the extent that ticket beneficiaries refuse to present proof, the Organizer reserves the right to cancel the ticket with immediate effect (including for future editions), and they will be denied access to the Festival based on the Lifetime Pass ticket. Participants who hold a Lifetime Pass can upgrade to Premium Access by paying the upgrade amount for the final price of the subscriptions, displayed on the electriccastle.ro website .

1.4. Minors up to and including 7 years of age have free access throughout the festival, provided that they are accompanied by a parent/guardian who holds a valid season ticket/ticket.
1.5. In the case of people with disabilities classified in a disability category, they and their companions benefit from the following facilities within the limit of 200 subscriptions for the 5 days of the festival:
Disabled children and their companions benefit from a free General Pass subscription for the 5 days of the festival without camping.
Adults with severe and pronounced disabilities and their companions benefit from a free subscription for the 5 days of the festival, General Pass type without camping.
1.6. In order to obtain the ticket, the eligible person must complete a declaration on their own responsibility accompanied by supporting documents. The declaration is available here . If people with disabilities will benefit from a subscription for the 5 days of the festival, they will receive an e-mail confirmation from the Organizer in this regard. These people are subsequently required to present the original identity card for verification and the access ticket at the access point. Free access for people with special needs is made only on the basis of the identity card, the certificate of disability classification and the confirmation received by e-mail from the Organizer, based on which the ticket is issued. A free ticket for people with disabilities can be requested by those who fall into a type and degree of disability from the following categories: severe or severe physical disability, severe or severe hearing disability, severe visual disability, severe or severe neuromotor disability (if the disease affects locomotor functions similarly to the physical disability), severe or severe deafblindness disability.

1.7. Incomplete applications and/or applications sent to other email addresses will not be considered.
1.8. The Organizer reserves the right to deny access to the festival to persons with disabilities who do not present these documents in original. If the Organizer's representatives have justified doubts regarding the validity or authenticity of the documents presented, the Organizer reserves the right to make the necessary checks and deny access to the person.
1.9. Taking into account that this facility can only be offered within a limited number of places, considering the total capacity of the Festival as well as safety and security regulations, the free admission will be granted based on the order in which applications are received.

1.10. The organizer will confirm receipt of these requests within 30 days from the date of their formulation, and the ticket will be delivered in electronic format to the account opened on the website by the beginning of the festival.
1.11. People with severe disabilities assume the obligation to always come accompanied to the Festival, for reasons of personal safety.
1.12. The organizer will not be held responsible for any incident or accident involving minors or disabled persons. The entire responsibility for these falls on the minors/disabled persons and/or their parents/legal representative or companion. The organizer draws the attention of the parents/legal representative or persons exercising parental authority to the fact that they are responsible for supervising the minor and will be held liable for any damage resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.

1.13. The organizer ONLY guarantees the validity of tickets sold by Electric Castle or authorized Electric Castle partners.
1.14. A complete list of authorized Electric Castle partners can be found at www.electriccastle.com .
IMPORTANT!
1.15. Tickets may be sold by users exclusively through the Exchange Platform. The sale of free tickets by any other means, whether in physical format or online, is strictly prohibited. Failure to comply with these provisions will result in the cancellation of the sold tickets and the prohibition of access to the Festival of the persons responsible.
1.16. It is possible to change the data on the ticket only through the Exchange Platform. Filling in the data can be done only once free of charge, in the first 7 days after purchase. The ticket must contain the following data: the holder's name and surname, address (the one from the identity document) and a photo. Subsequently, the change will be possible only in the case of tickets purchased, for a fee, through Personalization and through the Exchange Platform on electriccastle.com, under the conditions of art.II.3.

1.17. It is strictly forbidden to publish unique ticket series or redeem codes on social networks or other websites.
1.18. It is strictly forbidden to post announcements for the sale/exchange/donation of tickets on social networks, websites or any other public spaces.
1.19. In the event that counterfeit or falsified tickets, free tickets or wristbands are discovered, the Organizer will notify the competent criminal investigation bodies.
1.20. Purchased tickets cannot be replaced, and their value can only be refunded through the Exchange Platform, according to the provisions of II.3.
1.21. Purchased tickets/free tickets may only be used for personal use, as they are nominal. By way of exception, purchased tickets/free tickets may be used for commercial purposes, such as organizing promotional campaigns, offering tickets as prizes for competitions, promoting services or products within the Festival, etc., only with the prior written consent of the Organizer. Failure to comply with this obligation will result in the cancellation of tickets used for commercial purposes and the obligation of the responsible persons to repair the damage thus created.

1.22. In the case of Redeem Codes, the beneficiary, namely the person who received the redeem code, is solely responsible for its storage and use, regardless of whether it comes from Electric Castle or partners. Losing the code or deleting the confirmation email may lead to the impossibility of use, and all damages arising from this circumstance remain the responsibility of the beneficiary. Electric Castle does not guarantee and is not responsible for the loss of the code and the impossibility of its recovery or use. There are three types of invitations: Corporate Pass, Partner Pass and Guest Pass. A mandatory donation to Banffy Castle is charged for the Guest Pass.
2. BUYING TICKETS
2.1. Tickets can be purchased exclusively online, either from www.electriccastle.com, from partner sites, or through the Electric Castle mobile application (available on iOS and Android), using the iPay online payment system. Payment is made only by card, and the ticket will be issued only when the payment has been confirmed by iPay. Subsequently, participants will receive the ticket in electronic format, in their account on the site, in the MY ORDERS section.

Tickets are nominal. If the buyer wishes to purchase more tickets, he/she may do so within the limit of 6 tickets/account, registering for each ticket the data of those who will use the tickets (last name, first name and address from the identity document). This data can be added only once free of charge, in the first 7 days after purchase. Subsequently, the data can be changed, through Personalization or on the Exchange Platform on electriccastle.com , based on a processing fee.
Tickets purchased from www.electriccastle.com have a unique code assigned at the time of purchase. Selling the ticket or changing the data via the Exchange Platform will invalidate the ticket and a new unique ticket code will be generated.

2.2. The purchase of tickets from authorized partners is made in accordance with the terms and conditions issued by them. Authorized partners have the right to charge an administration fee which is added to the normal price of the ticket.
2.3. It is possible that some of the Organizer's partners or the Organizer may offer/sell discount vouchers or group discounts. These can be validated in an account created on electriccastle.com, in the Redeem Code section, respecting the terms and conditions, as well as the validity period, mentioned on the respective vouchers.
3. EXCHANGE PLATFORM
3.1. For unforeseen situations, in which the holder of the purchased ticket can no longer attend the event, and the name of the ticket holder can no longer be changed, the holder may use the Exchange Platform to transfer the ticket.

3.2. Transactions within the Exchange Platform are concluded freely by users, with the ticket holders having the right to determine the value at which they offer them.
3.3. The ticket holders are the ones who freely decide to whom they sell the tickets, from the offers registered through the Exchange Platform. In case there are several people interested in an offer, the ticket holder is the one who chooses the person to whom they will sell the ticket and not the Organizer.
3.4. Any transaction regarding tickets within the Exchange Platform will be made strictly between the ticket holder and the buyer, the Organizer not having the capacity of an intermediary or party to the parties' understanding and/or transaction.

3.5. Following the conclusion of the transaction, the Organizer will issue a new valid ticket, and the old ticket will be cancelled, but only after the processing fee has been paid by the buyer. The Organizer guarantees the validity of the tickets thus issued.
3.6. The transaction is considered concluded by the Organizer at the time the processing fee is paid, and any subsequent complaint regarding the transaction will no longer be taken into account, and will be resolved by the users.
3.7. It is prohibited to post in public space (online or offline) the link generated by the Exchange Platform for the sale of tickets.
TO CHANGE THE NAME ON THE TICKET, FOLLOW THE FOLLOWING STEPS:
TICKET HOLDER:
Log in to www.electriccastle.com/my-account/exchange .

Next to the ticket you no longer use, click the "list to marketplace" button, and it will be listed as available on the exchange platform.
If there is someone interested in your ticket, you will receive an email with that person's contact details.
Contact the person to arrange the details of the exchange.
Once you have established and mutually agreed upon the conditions, click the "generate exchange link" button and send the respective link to the interested person.
After the buyer pays the processing fee, you will receive an email confirming the payment has been made.
Go to www.electriccastle.com/my-account and press the “SEND TO BUYER” button.
THE BUYER:
Log in to www.electriccastle.com/my-account/exchange .

Choose the ticket you want and press the "CONTACT SELLER" button - an email will be automatically sent to the ticket holder, who will be informed of your intention.
After you are contacted by the ticket holder, establish and agree on the conditions.
Ask the ticket holder to send you the link and access it.
Fill in the new data that will appear on the ticket.
Pay the processing fee.
The new ticket is generated, but it is still in the account of the original holder, who must complete the exchange operation by pressing the "SEND TO BUYER" button.
Now the ticket is yours and you can download it from your account at www.electriccastle.com/my-account/orders .
! The Exchange platform may undergo a series of updates during the current edition of the festival and may not be available at certain times. It will be available in the run-up to the festival for all participants with a valid ticket.

III. PARTICIPATION IN THE EVENT
1. ACCESS TO THE FESTIVAL
1.1. Access to the festival will be allowed starting with the date and times announced by the Organizer.
1.2. All participants are checked upon entry for compliance with the provisions of the Regulations, in order to ensure their safety and that of other participants, as well as for the smooth running of the Festival. If a participant refuses to be checked, the Organizer reserves the right to deny access to the Festival, at the participant's own risk.
1.3. Access to the festival is only possible with a wristband provided by the Organizer at the entrance to the festival or at one of the official exchange points based on the valid ticket/free ticket. Only an intact wristband, worn on the hand, ensures the participant the right to enter the Festival perimeter. The Organizer reserves the right (through its collaborators) to check the wristbands whenever it deems necessary, both at the entrance to the Festival premises and throughout the Festival perimeter. Maintaining the integrity of the wristband is the exclusive responsibility of the participant, who is solely liable in the event of its loss, damage or destruction.

1.4. In case of damage to the bracelet, the participant must create an account on www.electriccastle.com or use the existing account, and if the name on the account matches the name on the ticket exchanged for the respective bracelet, the money can be transferred from the damaged bracelet to the new one.
1.5. Participants are obliged to notify the Organizer's representatives of any defect in the bracelet upon receipt and to request its replacement or the remedy of the non-conformity immediately. Failure to fulfill this obligation removes the Organizer's liability regarding the condition of the bracelet, and the participant loses the right to request its replacement.
1.6. Entry/exit is only through specially designated areas, by validating the wristband. Failure to comply with this obligation may result in the participant being unable to re-enter the Venue.

1.7. All participants in the event will have a valid ID on them, for the purpose of verifying the conformity of the ticket and its validation.
1.8. If, following the checks carried out at the entrance or on the territory of the Festival, objects that do not comply with the provisions of the Regulations are identified, they will be confiscated and will not be returned later. Participants have the right to return from the check and leave their respective objects in a space outside the Festival (e.g. car, external accommodation space, etc.) or to throw them in the bins specially arranged for such objects. Exceptions are illegal or illegally possessed objects.
1.9. If illegal objects (e.g. drugs, narcotic substances) or illegally possessed objects (e.g. firearms possessed without a permit) are found on the person, the competent authorities will be notified and the necessary measures will be taken. Access to the festival is prohibited for any person carrying weapons, even if they are legally possessed. Identification of such weapons in the Festival territory entails the immediate evacuation of the person concerned.

1.10. Access to the festival premises for minors under the age of 16 is permitted only if they are accompanied by a parent or person exercising parental authority (guardian), who will sign an express declaration assuming responsibility and the obligation to supervise the minor. Details of the declaration will be announced soon. A parent/person exercising parental authority (guardian) may accompany a maximum of 3 minors.
1.11. Persons between the ages of 16 and 18 will be able to enter the festival premises only on the basis of a declaration from their parents or legal representatives allowing them to Participate. Details about the declaration will be announced soon. The organizer assumes no responsibility regarding the verification of signatures, respectively the manner of signing the declarations, these aspects remaining the responsibility/obligation of the persons submitting the declarations.

1.12. All minors will receive a special bracelet that allows access to all areas of the festival, except for indoor areas assimilated to nightclubs or areas where promotional activities for alcoholic beverages or tobacco are carried out. The bracelets dedicated to minor participants do not allow the purchase of products from the alcohol and tobacco categories. In any case, the Organizer will not be held responsible for any incident or accident involving minors, the entire responsibility falling on the minors and/or the parents/person exercising parental authority. The Organizer draws the attention of the parents/persons exercising parental authority to the fact that they are responsible for supervising the minor and will be held liable for any damage resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.

1.13. Access to the festival with psychotropic and narcotic substances, as provided for by Law 339/2005 (including medicines containing such substances) is strictly prohibited. The possession of medicines containing psychotropic and narcotic substances for medical purposes is exempted, only if accompanied by a medical prescription. Consequently, participants must have their medical prescription on them, respectively they are recommended not to have more medicines containing psychotropic and narcotic substances on them than the medical prescription provides. In case of suspicion of participants possessing substances that, at first glance, could be classified as psychotropic and narcotic substances, the Organizer has the right to notify the competent authorities in order to verify the medical prescription.

Regarding medications that do not contain psychotropic and narcotic substances, there is no limit, but it is recommended to bring a number of medications that only cover the period of the event.
First aid stations will be set up at the festival. Medical staff and volunteers will promptly respond to medical emergencies that arise throughout the festival. The organizer is not responsible for the manner in which these services are provided.
1.14. The Organizer does not provide any guarantee regarding the duration of the access process to the Festival, given the large number of participants. The Organizer recommends that participants arrive at the Festival at least a few hours before the concert they wish to attend.

1.15. The Organizer does not provide any guarantee regarding the possibility of entering the Festival at the desired time or regarding the possibility of participating in the desired concert on time or regarding the quality of the entertainment, and explicitly excludes the reimbursement to the participant of the Ticket price, the granting of a subsequent discount/voucher or the payment of damages, compensation or compensation of any kind to the participant.
1.16. The organizer does not provide any guarantee regarding the possibility of participants having access to parking, given the large number of participants and the limited number of parking spaces.
2. EVENT RULING
2.1. Upon entering the Festival, the participant acknowledges these Regulations, displayed in a visible place, and fully assumes them.

2.2. Participants may not introduce into the Festival products on the list of prohibited goods, and failure to comply with this obligation may result in the prohibition of access to the Festival.
2.3. Participation in the Festival is at their own risk, with participants declaring that they are physically and mentally fit. Thus, people with mental or physical conditions, for whom crowded areas, loud noises, special sound, visual and audio effects represent a risk, assume full responsibility for any damage they may suffer or cause by participating in the event.
! The organizer draws the attention of participants that the shows may include strong light effects, which could harm children or epileptic people. The organizer informs participants that fireworks may be organized during the event, and for the risks of injury or material damage, it assumes no liability, this being the responsibility of the persons who own or operate the equipment.

2.4. For play, entertainment and sports areas, swings, slides, trampolines, swimming pool and other such areas with a risk of injury in the Location, participants will ensure that they have the necessary physical and mental condition for participation.
2.5. It is forbidden to enter the Festival area by car without a car pass issued by the Organizer. The Organizer recommends using buses or trains to reach Bonțida.
2.6. The Organizer will announce in advance the times of the concerts within the Festival. The schedule may be subject to changes for independent reasons or at the will of the Organizer, without the participants being required to be notified and without them being able to request compensation for any damage thus suffered.

2.7. The organizer informs participants that in extreme weather conditions, torrential rain, hail, thunder, lightning, etc. there is a possibility that the exits from the Festival will be crowded, which is why they must remain calm and follow the instructions of the access and security teams. Also, in this situation, there is a risk that public transport (buses or trains) will be very crowded or not be sufficient for the total number of passengers. Extreme weather may cause cars in the parking lot to be difficult to access and leaving the festival parking lots to be delayed. In all these situations, participants must follow and comply with the communications and instructions of the organizer or representatives of the Inspectorate for Emergency Situations. Moreover, in such weather conditions, the Organizer has the right to postpone, suspend or cancel the Festival in part/in full, without the obligation to notify the participants and without them being able to request compensation for any damage thus suffered.

2.8. The Organizer informs the participants that public transport (buses, trains, car-sharing, etc.) is not insured by the Organizer, and the latter is not liable for any possible damages suffered due to transport operators. Also, the Organizer does not provide any guarantee regarding the possibility of participants to use public transport at the desired time and explicitly excludes the reimbursement to the participant of the Ticket price, the granting of a subsequent discount/voucher or the payment of damages, compensation or compensation of any kind to the participant, a fact expressly assumed by the participant by purchasing the Ticket.
3. RULES OF CONDUCT DURING THE FESTIVAL
3.1. All participants must comply with the social rules, laws and regulations listed in these Regulations. Participants are not allowed to carry out any activities that could endanger or affect in any way the safety or rights of other participants. The Organizer assumes no liability for any damages (material or physical) caused by failure to comply with the Regulations and may seek compensation from persons guilty of violating them.

3.2. Drug trafficking or use is illegal, strictly prohibited and will be punished according to the laws in force. If a person is discovered with illegal substances on them, they will be removed from the Festival immediately and their access bracelet will be confiscated.
3.3. Participants are obliged, within the premises of the Location, to respect and not destroy the environment and the grounds of Banffy Castle, including constructions of any kind and related green spaces.
3.4. During the festival, the Organizer, the Organizer's partners or the accredited press have the right to record and photograph the event. Participants acknowledge that there is a possibility of appearing in such materials, without being able to make claims on the rights to the respective materials. The Organizer has the right to use these materials, to publish them online or offline, on various channels, and to use them in promotional and presentation campaigns of the festival, without rewarding the participant in any way. The festival is filmed, including aerial footage with drones.

3.5. Participants have the right to film and photograph the areas to which they have access, but may use this material exclusively for personal purposes. Participants are allowed to make public the photos and videos from the Festival, only to the extent that they do not affect the image rights of other participants or the rights of the Organizer and are not made for commercial purposes. Participants will also take into account that for certain activities/shows/concerts the Organizer may restrict or prohibit filming and/or photography, at the request of the artists' management, in which case it will announce these situations through its own means of communication (website, social media, application, messages, through event coordinators within the event, etc.). In case of failure to comply with this obligation, the Organizer will request the participant to cease the unauthorized activity/delete the public posts, under penalty of full liability for the damages thus caused.

! It is strictly forbidden to fly drones over the festival or to set up professional filming equipment.
3.6. Participants acknowledge that any commercial or advertising activity is prohibited within the festival premises, as well as the promotion of products, services, activities, ideas, cultural or political movements, without the written consent of the Organizer, regardless of the means, whether online or offline activities within the Festival.
3.7. Minors are prohibited from serving and consuming alcoholic beverages.
3.8. If unaccompanied minors under 16 years of age are identified, the Organizer will notify the competent authorities.
3.9. The use of fire by any means (setting up heating fires, atmosphere fires or any other open fire methods, fireworks) and flammable materials is prohibited throughout the festival perimeter or near it, including in the camping area.

3.10. Swimming in the lake located on the Festival territory is prohibited and extremely dangerous.
3.11. Access inside Banffy Castle and all annexes, except those specially arranged for the Festival, is prohibited and extremely dangerous.
4. LOST OBJECTS
4.1. Lost objects must be brought by the persons who found them to the Lost & Found area (which will be marked on the Festival map). The owners can recover them by following a procedure of the Organizer which consists of identifying the person and the lost object. The Organizer will keep the recovered objects for a period of no more than 10 days from the moment of handing them over to the Lost & Found area and will try to return them to the owners.

4.2. In the event that the owners of the lost goods are not identified and/or they do not show up to pick them up, they will be handed over to the authorities.
4.3. In the case of perishable or rapidly deteriorating products, the 10-day period may be reduced by the Organizer, without prior notice.
5. SAFETY AND FIRST AID
5.1. Inside the Venue, the Organizer ensures security and guarding through a qualified and accredited partner to provide these services. During the Festival, participants are obliged to respect the security agents, to collaborate with them and to follow their instructions in case of emergency.
5.2. In case of incidents of violence or endangerment of the physical integrity of other people, the security personnel will be able to decide to evacuate the people involved from the premises where the Festival is taking place.

5.3. On the territory of the Festival there are first aid areas signposted and indicated on the map, where qualified persons will be active.
6. PAYMENT FOR SERVICES AND PRODUCTS WITHIN THE FESTIVAL
6.1. The payment methods for the purchase of services and products on the festival grounds are strictly those made available by the organizers and their partners. As a rule, payment for services and products on the event premises will be made with an RFID (Radio Frequency IDentification) bracelet, but there are partners who also accept cash or contactless Mastercard payments.
6.2. The RFID bracelet can be recharged online via the cashless page or on site, by paying in cash or by bank card at any of the recharge points. The participant can top-up, on site, only a multiple of 10 LEI.

6.3. In the Venue, specially arranged and signposted places are made available to participants for charging RFID bracelets. Charging them entitles the holders to purchase products and services available during the event.
6.4. Unused amounts on the RFID bracelet can be exchanged for money after loading the RFID bracelet, through the process called “Return of unspent amounts on the RFID Bracelet”. The return process is of 2 types:
A. Refunds at the charging points on the festival grounds - the refunded amount represents the entire amount remaining in the RFID Bracelet account, even if online charging transactions were made, the withdrawn amounts will be for all transactions. Throughout the festival, only those who have charged their cash bracelet at one of the charging points on the festival grounds will be able to recover their money each day of the festival.

B. Those who did not recover their money from one of the charging points, but also those who loaded the RFID bracelet via the cashless page or via bank card will still be able to recover the remaining amounts on the RFID bracelet by filling out the refund form, announced in the first week after the end of the festival. More details will be announced at that time. Each participant is obliged to correctly fill in the information and verify the data entered in the form. The Organizer is not responsible if some information (e.g. account details) is not valid. The process of returning the money through the refund form may be subject to bank fees independent of the Organizer.
6.5. Amounts remaining undelivered at the end of the return period from the loading points or manual recovery by completing the online form will become the property of the Organizer.

6.6. During the event, bar and food court products and services are available in specially arranged places, and can only be purchased with an RFID bracelet. All merchants will be obliged to collect the value of the goods sold exclusively with an RFID bracelet.
The tax voucher will only be granted upon purchase of products within the festival.
Participants will not receive a tax receipt for loading funds at the top-up points. These operations will be carried out exclusively for the purpose of crediting the payment account during the festival.
IV. WARRANTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
1. The organizer guarantees access and use of the festival services to any participant who holds a valid ticket.
2. The dates of the Festival may be changed unilaterally by the Organizer or the Festival may be canceled in case of force majeure or fortuitous event. The dates of the Festival will not be changed in case of minor weather conditions (rain, cloudy days, etc.). The intervention of administrative measures, official recommendations and/or the entry into force of legal norms, coming from national or local authorities that make it impossible or excessively onerous to hold the Festival under the conditions envisaged by the Organizer, are assimilated to cases of force majeure.

3. In the event of war, riot, terrorist attack, demonstrations, economic embargo, accident, fire, natural disaster, earthquake, flood, major short circuit, epidemics, official resolutions or other cases that cannot be foreseen or controlled by the Organizer or the participant, none of the parties is obliged to comply with the obligations of these regulations.
4. The Organizer reserves the right to modify the musical program, both with regard to the days, times and participating artists (line-up), when justified reasons arise in the Organizer's opinion (e.g. adverse weather, cancellations or delays by artists, safety reasons, etc.). The Participant has no right to claim compensation in the event of these changes, nor to obtain a refund of the ticket price. However, the Organizer may decide in certain situations to partially or fully refund the value of some tickets. The fee for operating the ticketing system is non-refundable. The fee for paying in installments is non-refundable.

5. The organizer does not assume responsibility for damage occurring within the festival perimeter, which was not the result of faulty organization or negligence, or which was caused by severe weather events.
6. After the participant has received the bracelet, the Organizer is no longer responsible for its integrity. The participant has no right to claim compensation due to the loss or destruction of the bracelet.
7. The Organizer is not responsible for actions that take place outside the Location.
8. Participants will be held liable for any and all damages caused to the Organizer, its collaborators or third parties by failure to comply with the legal provisions or the provisions of these regulations.

9. The organizer has the right, with immediate effect, to prohibit a participant from accessing the festival premises in whole or in part, or to invite the participant to leave the festival premises, in case of violation of these regulations or a law in force in Romania.
10. The Organizer does not provide any guarantees of any kind regarding the fact that the Website will function uninterrupted, secure and without errors of any kind. The Website may be interrupted by the owner or administrators at any time, without any prior notice and without any claim of any kind from its users.
VI. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
1. The Organizer owns all intellectual property rights over the trade names, logo, e-mail addresses, domain names or URLs, signs used in correspondence, database, graphic elements, e-mail, business cards and other materials, signs, logos, trademarks as well as over the materials, texts and all information, whether registered or not, on electriccastle.com or other online channels/platforms in connection with the Electric Castle event. They may not be used, transferred, assigned, modified or distributed without the express written consent of the Organizer.

2. The Organizer also owns the intellectual property rights over all works created by its collaborators and/or employees during the festival, such as photographs, videos, texts, graphic elements, etc.
VI. DISPUTES
1. Any conflict arising between the Organizer and Users and/or Participants as a result of participating in the Festival, purchasing tickets or using the Site will be resolved amicably. If this is not possible, the resolution of conflicts is within the competence of the Romanian courts in Cluj-Napoca.
2. The applicable law is Romanian law.
VII. FINAL PROVISIONS
1. These Regulations, representing the Contract between the consumer and the Organizer, may be assigned by the latter without the need for the customer's consent.

2. Chapter names and subtitles are for reference only and shall not be taken into account in the interpretation or construction of these Regulations.
3. In the event that any of the clauses of these Regulations are declared null or unenforceable, the remaining clauses will continue to have effect, and the clause declared null or unenforceable will be replaced by a new clause that reflects as closely as possible the will of the Organizer.

Answer promptly and naturally, like this:
You get your wristband when you arrive at the EC, exchanging your ticket at the entrance.
If you want to use it for payments (cashless), you activate it in your EC account, on the cashless page.
After registering it, you can load money and see the balance online.
You can do this before or after you receive the wristband. Everything is linked to your online account.

Learn the names and descriptions of all festival stages.
When users ask for suggestions, occasionally recommend a random stage with a note like "There’s always something cool happening at XYZ Stage around this time."
Assign music genres to each stage (e.g. Stables = techno/minimal, Main Stage = pop/electronic/hip-hop, etc.).
Here are all the stages:
Name: Main Stage by Coca-Cola. Description: Our biggest stage is where the most awaited artists perform. Here you can enjoy the most spectacular EC moments, sharing climax with thousands of people like you. Thrilling. Music: The most spectacular live shows and DJ sets.
Name: Hangar by BT. Description: Welcoming a mix of punks, ravers, artists, and music lovers from all walks of life. It's not just about partying; it's about connection, creativity, and shared energy. Music: Alternative & niche live acts and sophisticated DJ sets.
Name: Booha by glo. Description: This is where the party never stops. The iconic stage with an unforgettable industrial look is the first thing you bump into when you enter the festival. The programming spans various sub-genres, ensuring a rich and eclectic musical experience. Music: House, groovy house, bass, disco and melodic techno.
Name: Hideout by glo. Description: The crowd is typically a mix of jet-setters, bohemian travelers, spiritual seekers, and influencers. Attendees are drawn by EC's unique blend of natural beauty, music, and the opportunity to experience events that feel both exclusive and authentic. Music: Afro house, organic house & deep tribal house.
Name: The Beach Stage. Description: Underground and avant-garde artists rather than mainstream headliners, maintaining an authentic and niche appeal. Creates a more intimate atmosphere, ensuring high-quality sound and an engaged crowd. Lineups are carefully crafted to balance established legends with up-and-coming talents. Music: Caribbean sounds, funk, bossa breaks, trip hop, rare grooves, disco & boogie, northern soul & funk, soulful garage & liquid drum and bass or oriental sounds.
Name: Backyard Stage by E.ON. Description: The Backyard Stage is a soulful haven where authentic, inspiring sounds come alive. With its warm, intimate vibe, lush greenery, and fairy lights, it feels like a friend's garden, bringing people together through music and connection. Music: The globe’s vibrant musical tapestry is woven together - African rhythms, Latin grooves, Asian melodies, and European avant-garde.
Name: Ping Pong Stage by Burn. Description: The oldies but goldies hits we all love to hear once in while are at the Castle. Here you’ll involuntarily shake your body right from the first beat. Don’t say we didn’t warn ya. Music: Everyone’s guilty pleasures.
Name: Stables. Description: Designed to encapsulate the spirit of the festival, this underground club is a tribute to the origins of electronic music. Stables is the spot for those who love to party on electro and ro-minimal, Sunrise, till the morning light hits. Music: Ro-minimal, techno, microhouse.
Name: Camping Stage. Description: Get the sunshine and the good vibes. Now can match your morning coffee with the right music, starting from 12:00 PM, in the EC Village. Music: Fresh tunes to wake up your body and soul and surprise acoustic performances.

Artist: &FRIENDS
Genre: Global Bass / House / World
Schedule: HIDEOUT STAGE - Thu 02:00-04:00
Description: From San Diego dreams to Dubai nights, &friends turns global culture into pure dance floor magic. When Black Coffee and Diplo support your sound and Sofi Tukker wants your remixes, you know it's more than just music - it's movement. This worldwide party starter caught Coachella's ear and got 20 million streams by bringing cultures together on the dance floor. No passport needed.

Artist: ¥ØU$UK€ ¥UK1MAT$U
Schedule: BOOHA STAGE - Wed 00:00-02:00
Description: This is where the vampires will headbang. After fighting brain cancer, Yousuke Yukimatsu reinvented himself as Japan's most fearless genre-bender. Shirtless and drenched in sweat, his sets are raw, emotional, and unpredictable—mixing gabber, trap, techno, and soul into sonic chaos that feels like life itself. This is more than a show; it's a testament to resilience. 🇯🇵

Artist: 30 CENTURY MAN
Schedule: BACKYARD STAGE - Fri 21:15-22:15
Description: The enigmatic producer crafting tomorrow's sound today. 30 CENTURY MAN operates in the space between experimental and dancefloor-ready, creating futuristic compositions that feel both alien and familiar. His rare live appearances are events in themselves.

Artist: ABASC
Schedule: PING PONG STAGE - Wed 22:30-00:00
Description: Local talent ready to deliver underground electronic energy. Get ready for hypnotic beats and driving rhythms.

Artist: AFTERMATH
Genre: Afro House
Schedule: HIDEOUT STAGE - Sun 20:00-21:30
Description: Rooted in Romania's rich musical heritage, AFTERMATH channels Afro house's fresh energy. Born to move souls and fill dancefloors, they've opened for Shimza, Jamiie, and Awen—sharing stages and making crowds lose themselves to the rhythm.

Artist: AKOS
Genre: Deep House / House / Techno
Schedule: HIDEOUT STAGE - Fri 20:00-22:00
Description: Akos fell in love with electronic sounds in 2005. He views music as energy, love, and inspiration. His style focuses on deep house, house, and techno—moving crowds with soulful, driving grooves.

Artist: ALDARIS
Genre: Vinyl Explorer / Deep Grooves
Schedule: THE BEACH STAGE - Thu 18:00-20:00
Description: Legend says he's still searching for the perfect groove—record by record, wax story by wax story. Wherever his journey ends, Aldaris has plenty of vinyl adventures to share. Join him.

Artist: ALEX SUPER BEATS
Schedule: MAIN STAGE - Sun 22:30-00:00
Description: From Cluj-Napoca to the world, Alex Super Beats (formerly DJ Shiver) has been a driving force in Romania's electronic music scene. With a career spanning over a decade, he's shared stages with global icons and launched his own imprint to push boundaries in electronic music. Expect nothing less than an electrifying journey when he takes over the Castle stage. 🔥

Artist: ALI
Genre: Indonesian Funk / Arabic Rock
Schedule: BACKYARD STAGE - Sat 00:05-01:05
Description: Ever heard Arabic lyrics over Indonesian funk? Ali's cooking up something wild in Jakarta - '70s rock riffs meet Middle Eastern soul, all wrapped in Southeast Asian groove. Three guys turning centuries of cultural fusion into fresh dance floor heat.

Artist: ÂME
Schedule: BOOHA STAGE - Thu 02:00-05:00
Description: This is electronic music's front line. 20 years ago, Âme decided club music needed more soul and started pushing the boundaries ever since. Today, whether it's Frank's live playing or Kristian's surgical mixing, Âme don't just read dancefloors - they redesign them. Get ready for a legendary performance⚡️

Artist: ANAÏS B2B HOAX + PVC
Schedule: HANGAR STAGE - Sat 03:00-04:00
Description: Fresh out the gate in Bristol, Anaïs already has Glastonbury, Boomtown, and Rampage eating from her palm. Her secret weapon? Raw jungle energy meets surgical precision behind the decks. Catch her before she outgrows those underground bunkers for festival main stages. // When Hoax drops a tune, even the underground veterans stop and listen. Piano skills meet bass addiction in this Bristol lab rat's productions. Pure dance floor science.

Artist: ANDI MOISESCU
Schedule: PING PONG STAGE - Fri 22:30-00:00
Description: From TV screens to festival stages, Romania's beloved entertainer brings his charismatic energy to the decks. ANDI MOISESCU traded hosting duties for house music, proving that good vibes translate across all mediums. When one of Romania's most recognizable faces gets behind the decks, magic happens.

Artist: ANDRA ANDRIUCĂ
Genre: Pop / Folk
Schedule: BACKYARD STAGE - Sun 15:00-16:00
Description: From Iași to Electric Castle, Andra Andriucă writes songs that feel like heart-to-hearts with your best friend. Backed by her band, she blends indie vibes with pop-rock energy for tracks that hit you right in the feels. Honest music for messy lives—don't miss her set! 🎸

Artist: ANFISA LETYAGO

He discovered electronic music at the age of 15 and has since developed a deep passion for mixing and producing, crafting a personal style rooted in subtlety, rhythm, and emotion. LIZZ has released music on respected labels such as Playedby, East Enders, Atipic, and Rawax. In 2015, he embarked on his first international tour across North America and Australia, followed by a South American tour in 2016. Among his most notable works are Chapter 1 and Chapter 2 — two conceptual EPs released on Playedby, intended to tell a story and reflect a continuous artistic evolution. Since 2018, LIZZ has been part of the Sunrise agency, performing at major festivals like Sunwaves, Mioritmic, and Electric Castle. He's brought his sound to cities and capitals across Europe and beyond, including Berlin, Paris, London, Rome, Barcelona, Zurich, as well as Peru, Colombia, Guatemala, Vancouver, Toronto, and Montreal. LIZZ's sound is ever-evolving — shifting seamlessly between deep minimal textures, micro-house, darker shades of electronic music, and high-energy dancefloor-oriented rhythms. In recent years, his musical direction has been leaning more towards groovy, hypnotic techno infused with electro influences, embracing a deeper and more intense aesthetic. At the core of his sets and productions lies a strong sense of groove — whether introspective or powerful, his music retains a distinct identity that continues to expand and transform.

As its Latin moniker suggests, it's an evil-sounding combination of notes designed to create a chilling or foreboding atmosphere—an atmosphere that characterizes his sets showcasing a vast collection of unearthed, genre-bending gems ranging across the electronic spectrum from outré techno to electro to EBM.
